curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: May the Force be with you.
quotes.speaker: Star Wars (1977)
quotes.context: Said during the Rebel briefing before the assault on the Death Star (and by Han to Luke).

quotes.text: You assume your incompetence is a secret, and yet it announces itself the moment you walk into the room.
quotes.speaker: The Devil Wears Prada (2006)
quotes.context: Miranda Priestly quietly dressing down Andy Sachs.
quotes.fake_note: Nails Miranda's icy, devastatingly articulate cruelty, but she traditionally attacks specific fashion choices (like the cerulean sweater) rather than dealing in broad workplace insults.

quotes.text: There are a lot of things I regret, Ilsa, but Paris isn't one of them.
quotes.speaker: Casablanca (1942)
quotes.context: Rick Blaine speaking to Ilsa in his office after hours.
quotes.fake_note: Perfectly captures Rick's cynical, world-weary cadence, but the subtle giveaway is that it contradicts his deeply sentimental attachment to their past ('We'll always have Paris').

quotes.text: You didn't build a park, John, you built a ticking clock.
quotes.speaker: Jurassic Park (1993)
quotes.context: Ian Malcolm lecturing Hammond during the Chilean sea bass lunch.
quotes.fake_note: Flawlessly mimics Malcolm's condescending doomsday warnings, but 'ticking clock' is standard action-movie jargon; the real Malcolm uses highly specific chaos theory metaphors.

quotes.text: Did we just become best friends?
quotes.speaker: Step Brothers (2008)
quotes.context: Brennan (Will Ferrell) to Dale (John C. Reilly) after they bond over shared interests; Dale replies "Yup!"

quotes.text: I didn't fly three thousand miles just to die in a ventilation shaft.
quotes.speaker: Die Hard (1988)
quotes.context: John McClane muttering to himself while crawling through the ductwork.
quotes.fake_note: Sounds exactly like McClane's sarcastic, self-deprecating humor during the vent scene, but it replaces his iconic, much more ironic line about coming to the coast for 'a few laughs'.
